column6: Makes clinical validation, A/B testing, and multi-sport adaptation painful. Move to a config.yaml with pydantic or dataclasses validation.

Opportunity: _Sports2DModule.process(config) runs synchronously in the main thread.
Why It Matters: Blocks UI/callbacks for 30-90s on long videos. Run in a subprocess or concurrent.futures with progress polling.

column4: Users think the pipeline hung on 4K/60fps videos. Add a simple callback interface or tqdm integration.

Opportunity: Manual string formatting for OpenSim files.
Why It Matters: Prone to spec drift. Use pandas + numpy.savetxt or the official opensim Python API for guaranteed compliance.

column5: Reduces clinical credibility. Parameterize with literature-backed defaults (Minetti, GaitLab, ISB standards) and expose via config.

Opportunity: Zero unit tests or mock data pipelines.
Why It Matters: Refactors (e.g., Kalman gain, TRC parsing) can silently break biomechanics. Add pytest with synthetic .mp4 + .trc fixtures.
